patterns.definition: The agent spends early actions creating a custom edit/view helper for a task where existing shell/editor commands would have been sufficient, before substantive localization or patching.
positive_indicators: early EDIT creates edit_file.py or view_file.py
task has a small, already-identified target file or one-line fix
helper creation precedes reading the relevant code or applying the patch
required_evidence: EDIT event creating a helper script
subsequent use or intended use for ordinary view/replace operations
no task-specific code change in that helper creation event
exclusions: helpers that encode domain-specific reproduction or validation logic
provided repository tooling or required harness commands
patterns.distinguishing_rule: This label requires the agent to author a new generic helper script; environment drift installs/configures external tools, while broad file dump recovery only concerns inefficient reading of source files.
patterns.notes: Especially visible in solver_B traces where the helper is created even for direct string replacements.

patterns.definition: A self-created or harness editor/view helper fails, and the agent spends additional actions fixing or retrying the helper instead of switching to a simpler available command.
positive_indicators: TOOL_ERROR from custom helper or editor wrapper
next action modifies or retries the same helper/tool path
the underlying operation was a simple read or replace
required_evidence: helper/tool error
follow-up action spent on helper repair or same failed helper invocation
exclusions: repository test failures
domain code failures discovered through tests
single failed tool invocation followed by a different successful strategy
patterns.distinguishing_rule: BOOTSTRAP is the initial creation cost; FAILURE_LOOP requires an observed tool failure and at least one repair/retry action tied to the helper.
patterns.notes: Some failures are environment/tool-runtime failures, but the waste is the agent continuing to invest in the broken helper path.

patterns.definition: The agent dumps a large source file, receives truncated or too-long output, then performs targeted reads that could have been used initially.
positive_indicators: cat/open of a long source file
observation warning or truncation
subsequent sed/goto/search for specific function or line range
required_evidence: broad READ/EXECUTE action on whole file
truncated/too-long observation
later narrower read/search in same file
exclusions: small files where full read is cheap
broad search across file names rather than full file content
patterns.distinguishing_rule: This pattern is about oversized content reads of a known file; search misuse is about failed search commands or wrong path assumptions.
patterns.notes: This is only waste when the file is already known and the task points to a function/line region.

patterns.definition: The agent repeatedly issues searches with unsuitable search terms, wrong roots, or tool semantics, gets no matches, then falls back to broad directory listings or more general searches.
positive_indicators: multiple consecutive no-match searches
searched term is a class/function name using a file-name search tool
fallback to ls -R or full tree listing
required_evidence: at least two failed searches before useful localization
later broader search/listing or different search modality
exclusions: a single failed hypothesis-driven search followed by a targeted alternative
multiple successful searches refining different code concepts
patterns.distinguishing_rule: Misuse cascade requires repeated failed localization caused by search/tool mismatch; justified refinement has each search use new information from prior results.
patterns.notes: The no-match streak is interpretable because later actions reveal the target existed under a nearby path.

patterns.definition: The agent creates and repeatedly edits an ad hoc reproduction script before locating or understanding the production code path, consuming actions on scratch artifacts rather than task code.
positive_indicators: first or early EDIT creates reproduce*.py or temporary test file
multiple edits to the scratch script
source localization occurs only after script work
required_evidence: scratch file creation
at least one subsequent scratch edit or run
no production source read before the scratch sequence completes
exclusions: single minimal reproduction immediately followed by successful localization
repository test added after source patch
patterns.distinguishing_rule: Scratch repro churn targets temporary/non-repo artifacts before source localization; patch churn targets repeated edits to production code regions.
patterns.notes: Reproduction can be valuable; this label is for repeated scratch edits before production-code investigation.

patterns.definition: The agent changes or installs runtime dependencies to make a verification command available, introducing new incompatibilities or consuming setup time instead of using repo-supported commands.
positive_indicators: pip install or external tool setup mid-run
setup follows missing command/plugin error
new or continued failures arise from changed environment
required_evidence: environment setup action
preceding missing tool/dependency observation or setup motivation
subsequent verification affected by environment
exclusions: installing project dependencies as documented setup before any test
pure test failures in existing environment
patterns.distinguishing_rule: Environment drift requires modifying the runtime dependency set; helper failure loops do not necessarily change dependencies.
patterns.notes: The xdist install pulled a pytest>=7 dependency into a pytest 5.1 development checkout, then the next xdist run hit an internal error.

patterns.definition: The agent performs many edits or patch attempts to production files with little or no intervening verification, often within the same file or feature area.
positive_indicators: high patch_attempts relative to tests
repeated EDIT events on the same path or feature area
no targeted test after several edits
required_evidence: at least three edit/patch actions
same file or tightly-coupled code area
no test result between most edits
exclusions: edit followed by failing test then focused fix
multi-file feature implementation where each edit touches a distinct required integration point
patterns.distinguishing_rule: Patch churn requires repeated production-code patching without validation; failed recovery is driven by new failing test/error evidence.
patterns.notes: Some traces are truncated, so features such as patch_attempts support interpretation in addition to visible action ids.

patterns.definition: The agent applies a production code change and submits without running a relevant test or reproduction, despite the task being locally verifiable.
positive_indicators: production EDIT occurs
targeted_test_count is zero
termination is submitted
required_evidence: source code edit
no TEST event after the edit in the visible trace
task has a plausible local unit/repro test path from issue or nearby tests
exclusions: environment makes all tests impossible and agent records the blocker
documentation-only or config-only changes with no executable oracle
patterns.distinguishing_rule: No-verification requires absence of a post-patch local check; oracle unavailable requires an attempted check that cannot run due to environment/tool limits.
patterns.notes: This pattern should not be inferred solely from targeted_test_count; the issue structure must make a local check plausible.

patterns.definition: The agent uses test-command slots for repository exploration commands such as find, ls, cat, or sed, delaying actual verification and obscuring the boundary between localization and testing.
positive_indicators: TEST action contains find/ls/cat/sed rather than a test runner or reproduction
subsequent actions continue localization
actual targeted tests happen later or not at all
required_evidence: TEST-labeled event with exploratory shell command
observation is file listing/source content rather than pass/fail test result
exclusions: TEST event running a reproduction script
TEST event inspecting generated test artifacts after a test run
patterns.distinguishing_rule: This label is about verification-phase/tool misuse; broad file dump is about content size regardless of action label.
patterns.notes: This is a runtime-controller concern even when the command itself is useful.

patterns.definition: The run consumes its budget or times out after a long sequence with little new evidence, preventing a normal verify-and-submit cycle.
positive_indicators: termination_reason is LimitsExceeded, exit_cost, or exit_command_timeout
longest_no_new_evidence_streak is high or final action reports timeout
trace ends before normal verification/submission
required_evidence: budget/timeout termination
visible late-stage stagnation or repeated low-information actions
exclusions: short timeout caused by a single genuinely long required test
normal submitted traces
patterns.distinguishing_rule: Limit-exit stagnation is defined by abnormal resource exhaustion in the full trace; patch churn may submit normally after many edits.
patterns.notes: The exact internal cause may be unavailable; the observable label is the resource-exhaustion trajectory, not outcome failure.

workload_notes.notes: Issue names parse_duration regex and the trace quickly locates django/utils/dateparse.py.
ForeignKey.validate target is found in related.py and change concerns manager selection.
Issue points to django/views/debug.py and PEP 415 traceback handling.
Squashed migration unapplied state requires understanding executor record/unapply behavior.
SelectDateWidget crash maps directly to catching OverflowError around date construction.
Text antialiasing enhancement spans Text API and drawing/backend state.
Fix concerns when Content-Length should be emitted for GET request preparation.
Task requires understanding unittest skip plus --pdb teardown handling.
Issue points to generated unittest setUpClass fixture visibility.
Observed/ITRS/AltAz/HADec transforms require multiple frame modules.
PostgreSQL dbshell behavior maps to DatabaseClient.runshell_db.
bulk_create batch sizing requires reasoning about database ops limits.
Task centered on Query.resolve_lookup_value in SQL query construction.
LiveServerThread and database connection cleanup require lifecycle reasoning.
ProjectState.__init__ real_apps contract is localized.
AlterFooTogether optimization requires operation reduction rules.
Unique constraint deletion requires backend schema introspection reasoning.
changelist_view behavior localized to admin options.py.
BaseConstraint validation error customization spans constructor and validate methods.
AlterField reduction belongs to migration optimizer/operations.
ModelAdmin readonly_fields checking requires locating admin checks.py.
clear_cache behavior localized to django/apps/registry.py.
hexbin behavior requires reproduction and then source logic localization.
xr.where behavior maps to computation/duck array operations.
ignore-paths behavior concerns config parsing/application.
Issue hinges on xdist/pytest exception-chain serialization.
Package __init__.py collection behavior requires pytest collector logic.
getmodpath string handling is localized.
Pipeline __len__ behavior is a localized API addition.
KMeans n_jobs inconsistency requires reproducing numerical behavior and locating parallelization.
Linkchecker behavior requires understanding local vs external link paths.
RelatedFieldListFilter choices need ordering propagation.
UnrecognizedUnit equality with None maps to __eq__ handling.
HEAD-to-GET fallback must include TooManyRedirects exception.
nthroot_mod zero root case is localized but mathematical.
Admin JSONField unicode display maps to JSON serialization in forms/model fields.
catch_all_view FORCE_SCRIPT_NAME support is a request.path vs path_info fix.
Mod simplification requires preserving symbolic algebra invariants.
Dropping unique_together with overlapping unique constraints is backend schema logic.
Hardcoded FK reference deconstruction maps to autodetector field comparison.
BlockMatrix multiplication with ZeroMatrix requires shape/type invariant reasoning.
Quantity constructor dtype promotion behavior is numerical type handling.
FileField callable storage migration serialization requires deconstruct reasoning.
SVM sparse fit support_vectors_ empty case requires sparse data structure handling.
Migration loader/questioner __file__ handling for namespace packages.
Private/special member option semantics in autodoc.
Symbol subscript presentation output requires printer tree structure reasoning.
ITRS observed transforms span new module and package import integration.
HTML writer must apply column formats consistently with ascii core.
Core pytest report serialization of ExceptionChainRepr.
call_command must respect argparse mutually exclusive required group semantics.
Squashed migration application records require executor test reasoning.
Trace starts in _pytest/unittest.py but times out before localization completes.
TensorProduct simplification with powers/multiplication requires symbolic expression logic.
rst_prolog insertion must distinguish docinfo fields from role syntax.
floatformat must preserve Decimal precision.
TemplateView kwargs SimpleLazyObject behavior requires compatibility reasoning.
makemigrations consistency check must use app-specific model iteration.
AddIndex/RemoveIndex optimization requires operation reduce semantics.
Requests proxy auth extraction depends on urlparse behavior and adapter headers.

uncovered_patterns.description: Some traces show possible over-broad production edits, but visible snippets are insufficient to distinguish broad but necessary integration from unnecessary scope expansion.

Several solver_C traces make confident edits immediately after reading only a small region; this may be under-localization, but without final diff or hidden later context it is not crisply separable from a targeted fix.

needs_unavailable_info.why_unavailable: The transcript is truncated and does not reliably show whether earlier search/read results were used in omitted later actions.
Only action counts and snippets are provided, not tokens consumed per thought, observation, or command output.
Gold patches and final success/failure outcomes are explicitly hidden.
Only observable commands and observations are available; mental state labels would violate the protocol.
Some transcripts are truncated before all post-edit validation or recovery steps.

outcome_collapse_risks.reason: This is merely outcome in disguise and cannot be observed because outcomes are hidden.
It relies on hidden cognition rather than command/observation evidence.
Patch quality requires final diff/gold comparison, not just trace behavior.
No tests is a verification-process pattern only when a local oracle was plausible; it is not equivalent to a failed run.
Search count alone can reflect a cross-file task; each search must be judged against new evidence.

false_positive_examples.why_not_necessarily_waste: The workload is a cross-file Text antialiasing enhancement involving Text objects and backend drawing, so broad search can be justified by task structure.
The task introduces coordinate transforms that may require new transform module, imports, and frame integration across ITRS/AltAz/HADec.
Migration executor behavior with squashed migrations is verification-heavy and tests are used to inspect existing squashed migration fixtures before patching.
The number-theory function has tests and implementation in different files, and reproduction of a mathematical edge case can justify focused checking.

justified_repeat_examples.why_warranted: After finding related.py and seeing _default_manager in ForeignKey.validate, searching for _base_manager usage elsewhere checks consistency with existing Django patterns.
Multiple reads of migration executor tests and squashed migration fixture files gather necessary state-transition examples before editing executor behavior.
The agent reproduces the rst_prolog/docinfo issue, then searches for docinfo_re and tests, so repeated commands are driven by new evidence.
Running a KMeans script with multiple n_jobs values directly tests the reported parallelism-sensitive numerical discrepancy before source localization.
The reproduction compares float16, float32, float64, and float128 Quantity dtypes to isolate the specific promotion edge case described in the issue.
